mental_model.intuition: Indices are fast forward, logs are rewind.
mental_model.description: Indices represent repeated multiplication. Surds are just indices in disguise (fractional powers). Logarithms are the inverse: asking 'what power do I need to raise the base to get this number?'

formula.statement: Indices: a^m x a^n = a^(m+n). a^m / a^n = a^(m-n). (a^m)^n = a^(mn). a^0 = 1. a^(-n) = 1/a^n. a^(1/n) = nth root of a. Surds: sqrt(a) x sqrt(b) = sqrt(a x b). sqrt(a) / sqrt(b) = sqrt(a/b). sqrt(a) + sqrt(b) != sqrt(a+b). Logs: log_a(x) means a^? = x. log_a(mn) = log_a(m) + log_a(n). log_a(m/n) = log_a(m) - log_a(n). log_a(m^n) = n x log_a(m). log_a(a) = 1. log_a(1) = 0. Base change: log_a(b) = log_c(b) / log_c(a). Flip: log_a(b) x log_b(a) = 1.
formula.explain: For indices, multiplying same-base terms adds exponents. For logs, base change allows converting to base 10 or base e. Surds merge under multiplication and division, but NOT under addition (sqrt(4+9) != sqrt(4)+sqrt(9)).

worked_example.problem: If log_2(32) = x and log_4(64) = y, find x + y.
steps: Sanity-check: log_4(64) via base change = log_2(64) / log_2(4) = 6 / 2 = 3. Correct.

shortcuts.title: Infinite Nested Roots
shortcuts.trick: For sqrt(a + sqrt(a + ...)), if 'a' is a product of two consecutive integers n x (n+1), the answer is (n+1).

shortcuts.title: Logarithmic Base Swap
shortcuts.trick: log_a(b) x log_b(a) = 1. You can freely flip base and argument by taking the reciprocal.

common_mistakes.title: Distributing roots over addition
common_mistakes.explanation: sqrt(a^2 + b^2) does NOT equal a + b. Roots can only be distributed over multiplication and division.

common_mistakes.title: Log of a sum
common_mistakes.explanation: log(a + b) does NOT equal log(a) + log(b). The product rule is log(a x b) = log(a) + log(b).

oa_questions.question: Find the value of x if log_3(x) + log_9(x) = 3.
oa_questions.approach: Change base: log_9(x) = log_3(x) / log_3(9) = log_3(x) / 2. So log_3(x) + 0.5 x log_3(x) = 3. 1.5 x log_3(x) = 3. log_3(x) = 2. x = 3^2 = 9.

oa_questions.question: Simplify sqrt(72) - sqrt(18)
oa_questions.approach: sqrt(72) = sqrt(36 x 2) = 6 x sqrt(2). sqrt(18) = sqrt(9 x 2) = 3 x sqrt(2). 6 x sqrt(2) - 3 x sqrt(2) = 3 x sqrt(2).
